commands.repair_reason: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because there are no actionable blocking findings from this review.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff changes Codex prompt routing and docs/tests only, without new dependencies, workflows, secrets handling, or broader tool access. commands.review_summary:
The PR moves native Codex skills metadata and the tool-routed workspace memory pointer from per-turn user prompt context into turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions while preserving fallback MEMORY.md prompt injection.

PR surface: Source +31, Tests +29, Docs +10. Total +70 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-backed rather than locally executed: current main assembles skills and the workspace-memory reference into the native Codex turn input, and the PR body gives a concrete two-turn gateway flow showing the repeated blocks before the fix.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because there are no actionable blocking findings from this review.

commands.author: lastguru-net

target.title: fix(codex): move skills list and memory pointer to collaboration instructions
target.body:
## Summary

This PR reduces repeated per-turn Codex prompt payload by moving two stable OpenClaw context blocks out of native Codex user input and into turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions:

- the compact OpenClaw skills list
- the tool-routed `OpenClaw Workspace Memory` pointer

Full `MEMORY.md` fallback injection is intentionally unchanged. When memory tools are unavailable, tools are disabled, or the active workspace differs from the configured memory workspace, `MEMORY.md` still uses the existing bounded turn-context path.

## Why

Native Codex threads preserve prior user turns in their transcript. OpenClaw was adding stable runtime/reference blocks to the user prompt every turn, so those blocks accumulated in the native Codex thread history, and bloated the context with duplicate content.

That is especially wasteful for the compact OpenClaw skills list:

- it is usually stable across normal turns
- it can be large
- it is metadata/instructions for how to load skills, not the user's actual request
- repeating it in user input makes prompt bloat worse after every turn

The `OpenClaw Workspace Memory` block has the same problem when `MEMORY.md` is routed through memory tools. It is a pointer telling Codex that durable memory exists and which tools to use. It does not need to live inside the repeated user prompt.

This matters because the user prompt should stay focused on the current user request and truly turn-specific reference context. Stable routing and reference context belongs in developer-instruction surfaces where Codex already handles collaboration-mode overlays.

## How

The change splits Codex prompt assembly more cleanly by lifetime and role:

- `OpenClaw Skills` is rendered as turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions for native Codex turns.
- The tool-routed `OpenClaw Workspace Memory` pointer is rendered as turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions.
- `buildCodexOpenClawPromptContext` no longer includes skills or the memory pointer in the per-turn user prompt wrapper.
- Full `MEMORY.md` fallback content is still included through the existing bounded workspace prompt context when the tool-routed memory path is not valid.
- Docs now describe the new native Codex routing behavior.

This keeps stable context available to Codex while reducing repeated user-input payload in normal native Codex sessions.

## Behavior addressed

Native Codex turns should stop repeating the compact OpenClaw skills list and tool-routed memory pointer in the per-turn user input. Those blocks should instead arrive through turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions. Full `MEMORY.md` fallback injection should continue to behave exactly as before.

## Real environment tested

Local OpenClaw source checkout, using the Codex app-server test harness. 

`bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts --run
git diff --check
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts docs/concepts/system-prompt.md docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md docs/reference/token-use.md`

## Evidence after fix

The Codex app-server tests now assert that:

- the OpenClaw skills block is present in `collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions`
- the skills block is absent from `turn/start` user input
- the tool-routed `OpenClaw Workspace Memory` pointer is present in collaboration developer instructions
- the memory pointer is absent from `turn/start` user input
- full `MEMORY.md` fallback content remains in the existing prompt path when memory tools cannot route it
- extra non-root `MEMORY.md` bootstrap files still use the normal workspace context behavior

The targeted test run passed:
text
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       124 passed (124)

## Observed result after fix

For normal native Codex turns with memory tools available, stable skills metadata and the memory-tool pointer are no longer repeated inside the user prompt. Codex still receives them as turn-scoped developer instructions, while the current user request remains clean.

When the full `MEMORY.md` fallback path is required, `MEMORY.md` remains in the bounded turn-context path as before.

## Real behavior proof guidance

Before the fix I ran the gateway, reset the session with `/new`, and prompted it first: `reply only "1"`. This step is needed as we want to check what happens to the prompt on the second turn. After the agent's reply, I entered the next prompt:
`check your current system prompt. report, do you have any duplicated blocks in there, like files being included multiple times`

Agent's reply:
```text
Yes. Current visible prompt has duplicated blocks now.

Core MD files:
- `TOOLS.md`, `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `USER.md` are still injected once each in developer context.
- `AGENTS.md` full content appears once, from the earlier user-provided `AGENTS.md instructions...` block.
- `MEMORY.md` and `HEARTBEAT.md` are not pasted.

Duplicated / repeated context:
- The `OpenClaw Skills` inventory is included again, effectively duplicated across the previous and current user runtime-context blocks.
- The `OpenClaw Workspace Memory` note is repeated.
- Conversation metadata blocks are repeated with new message ids/timestamps.
- The prior user question and my prior answer are present twice in the total visible context: once as actual conversation turns, and once quoted inside `OpenClaw assembled context for this turn`.

So: no full core MD file is duplicated, but yes, OpenClaw is now duplicating runtime/reference material and conversation history. The quoted conversation-context duplication is especially obvious.
```

After the fix, I did the same: reset the session with `/new`, and prompted it first: `reply only "1"`. After the agent's reply, I entered the same next prompt:
`check your current system prompt. report, do you have any duplicated blocks in there, like files being included multiple times`

Agent's reply now changed to reflect the expected behavior:
```text
Yes.

Visible duplicated/overlapping blocks in this turn:

- Previous turn transcript is duplicated: it exists in native conversation history and is also re-quoted inside `OpenClaw assembled context`.
- `skill-creator` appears twice as an available skill:
  - OpenClaw workspace skill: `~/src/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md`
  - Codex system skill: `/home/beru/.openclaw/agents/home/agent/codex-home/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md`
- There are two separate skill systems injected: OpenClaw workspace skills and Codex skills instructions. That is overlap, not exact file duplication.
- `AGENTS.md` is visible once from the earlier user-provided block in conversation history. I do not see it separately injected as a developer block.
- `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `USER.md`, and `TOOLS.md` each appear once in the loaded workspace context.

So the real current bloat is transcript duplication via `OpenClaw assembled context`, plus overlapping skill frameworks.
```

As expected, the agents does not see the addressed repetition anymore. It still identifies other duplications, but they are outside of the scope of this PR: skill overlap between Codex and OpenClaw, and conversation history bloat.
## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Native Codex turns stop repeating the compact OpenClaw skills list and the tool-routed OpenClaw Workspace Memory pointer in per-turn user input. Those blocks now arrive through turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions, while full MEMORY.md fallback injection stays on the existing bounded prompt path. Lightweight cron turns intentionally preserve the exact scheduled prompt and do not receive the skills overlay.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS for focused Codex app-server behavior tests, plus Blacksmith Testbox Linux Node 24 via `pnpm check:changed` for the changed extension/docs lanes.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
git diff --check
/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
pnpm check:changed
```

- Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the focused test run showed `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 124 passed (124)`. The final Blacksmith Testbox run was `tbx_01ksvtb706dcemdf9rzeq4fe6s` with GitHub Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26677310030, and completed with exit code 0. Autoreview completed clean with `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.
- Observed result after fix: The tests assert the OpenClaw skills block is present in Codex collaboration developer instructions, absent from `turn/start` user input, and recorded in trajectory `systemPrompt` rather than the user prompt. The tests also assert the routed memory pointer is delivered through collaboration developer instructions, full MEMORY.md fallback still uses the existing prompt path, ordinary delivery hints are rewrapped without leaking metadata into user text, and lightweight cron prompts stay byte-for-byte unchanged with no skills overlay.
- What was not tested: No live gateway/model session was rerun after the maintainer fix; the prior contributor live prompt transcript in this PR body remains supporting context only. No Telegram/Desktop channel behavior changed or was tested.
target.author: lastguru-net
